title: Serving Static Files
description: Serve CSS, images, and other static assets with express.static.

slides.title: express.static â€“ Serving Static Assets đź–Ľď¸Ź
slides.content:
# Static Files

`express.static` is built-in middleware to serve static files like HTML, CSS, images, etc.

```javascript
app.use(express.static('public'));
```

Now, files in the `public` directory are served at the root path. For example:
- `public/style.css` â†’ `/style.css`
- `public/images/logo.png` â†’ `/images/logo.png`

You can also mount multiple static directories or use a virtual path prefix:
```javascript
app.use('/static', express.static('public'));
// now files are served at /static/style.css etc.
```

slides.title: Example Structure
slides.content: Create a folder `public` with `index.html` and `style.css`. Then:

quizData.question: If you have `app.use(express.static('public'))` and a file `public/css/style.css`, how can you access it?
quizData.explanation: The static root is mapped to the URL root, so the path is `/css/style.css`.
